title: Decision record — pick new field name + v1-receipt stance
description: Lock in two protocol-shaping decisions BEFORE any code changes: (a) new wire field name to replace payload.narrow; (b) consumer behavior on receipt of a v1 payload after the v2 cut. Documented as a session-folder decision record. NO code changes — pure documentation. Recommended values: field=`entries`, stance=`silent-skip + one-line stderr breadcrumb`. User retains veto rights via outstanding_clarifications; veto only shifts implementation details inside downstream tasks, not the DAG.

files.change: Document chosen field name (`entries`) with 1-paragraph rationale citing existing local rebind in broad.cjs:443; document chosen v1-receipt stance (`silent-skip + breadcrumb`) with 1-paragraph rationale citing hook silent-exit-0 contract; list both decisions as outstanding_clarifications for user veto

implementation: Open packages/cli/templates/hooks/knowledge-hint-broad.cjs:437-443 to confirm the existing local rebind `const entries = ...` exists — this is the precedent for choosing `entries` as the wire name
Open packages/cli/templates/hooks/knowledge-hint-broad.cjs:464 comment to confirm the hook silent-exit-0 contract — this is the precedent for `silent-skip` v1-receipt stance
Write _protocol-v2-decisions.md with two sections: Decision 1 (field name) + Decision 2 (v1 stance), each with rejected alternatives and 3-bullet rationale

Add an Outstanding Clarifications section listing the user-veto window (field name veto only changes TASK-002/003/004/005 implementation, not DAG; stance veto only changes TASK-003/004/005 implementation)
Commit message: `docs(rc18): protocol v2 decision record — field=entries, v1-stance=silent-skip`

reference.examples: Mirror the structure of memory/project_grill_deferred_items.md decision sections — short, evidence-anchored, alternatives explicit

criteria: File `.workflow/.lite-plan/rc18-protocol-v2-2026-05-15/_protocol-v2-decisions.md` exists and contains both Decision 1 (field name = `entries`) and Decision 2 (v1 stance = silent-skip) with explicit rationale citing broad.cjs line numbers
Outstanding Clarifications section enumerates exactly 2 user-veto items with the impact-radius statement (which downstream tasks shift on veto)
Behavioral expectation: downstream task agents have an unambiguous answer to `what is the new field name?` and `what should v1-receipt do?` without re-deriving

Field-name consistency check: all 5 downstream tasks (TASK-002 through TASK-006) cite `entries` as the wire name in their convergence criteria — a grep of all .task/*.json for `payload.entries` returns ≥4 matches
Version-receipt stance check: documented stance (silent-skip + stderr breadcrumb) is testable via a unit test in TASK-005 asserting `renderSummary({version: 1, ...})` returns `[]`
Single git commit recorded with message starting `docs(rc18): protocol v2 decision record`

rationale.chosen_approach: Front-load both protocol-shaping decisions into a single docs-only commit so all 3 parallel implementation tasks (TASK-002/003/004) read from the same source-of-truth without race conditions
alternatives_considered: Inline decisions in commit messages of TASK-002
decision_factors: Parallel safety (TASK-002/003/004 must agree on names)
Auditability (decisions need a git-tracked artifact, not just commit messages)
Vetoability (user must see proposed values before code lands)
rationale.tradeoffs: Adds 1 task to the chain (6 commits instead of 5) for the upside of a documented pre-decision and zero ambiguity downstream

manual_checks: Open the decision record and confirm rationale references concrete broad.cjs line numbers (not vague claims)
Confirm Outstanding Clarifications section explicitly states what user-veto would change in downstream tasks
success_metrics: Both decisions documented with rejected alternatives explicit
User-veto impact radius stated for both decisions
